candidates.why: Clearly labeled as the top-line revenue figure; amounts are large and match expectation for total revenue.
Represents ancillary operating revenue, not the main total, but could serve as a fallback if Total Revenue were missing in some years.
A technical balancing line rather than an economic revenue measure; used only as last-resort fallback.

variables.notes: Using ordered priority list per instructions, with 'Total Revenue' as primary REVT.

candidates.why: Explicit COGS label on income statement; best match for direct production costs.
Inventory component, not period expense; not appropriate for COGS.
Balance-sheet PPE, not an expense.
Depreciation on PPE, not COGS as defined here.
Gross PPE balance, not expense.

variables.notes: There are duplicate 'Cost of goods sold' rows in the sheet, but the mapping uses the label as given; downstream logic can handle multiple occurrences.

candidates.why: This is revenue, not an operating expense; should not be included in SG&A.
Classic SG&A/overhead component covering personnel-related operating costs.
Broad operating overhead bucket; clearly SG&A-type expenses excluding COGS and D&A.
Likely an additional bucket of other operating overhead; fits SG&A definition.
Label suggests share-based compensation or related charge; treated as an SG&A-type overhead cost per instructions.

variables.notes: No single explicit SG&A total line; therefore multiple overhead component rows are selected per instructions. Revenue line is excluded.

candidates.why: Explicit R&D expense line, even if values are currently nan in preview; best semantic match for XRD.

variables.notes: R&D appears (possibly just as a placeholder) but should be mapped as XRD per rules that presence in sheet implies values in at least one year.

candidates.why: Net interest line; good fallback if specific interest expense lines are missing, but less preferred than gross interest expense.
Primary gross interest expense line; best match for XINT per instructions.
Appears to be additional/detail interest expense; good secondary choice after main Interest Expenses line.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; a relevant component of interest expense if main lines are unavailable.

candidates.row_label: Remeasurement of previously held equity interest
candidates.why: Relates to remeasurement gains/losses on equity interests, not interest expense.
Potential intra-group interest; could be part of interest expense but appears separate and may be netted elsewhere.
Represents allocation of profit, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity allocation, not interest.
Generic interest expense supplemental line; good fallback if populated in some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental detail of lease interest; lower-priority fallback.
Balance sheet liability, not period expense.
Liability balance, not interest expense.
Accrued but unpaid interest at period end; not the flow expense itself.
Accrued interest liability; again not the P&L expense line.
Long-term debt balance; not the expense.
Equity component, not interest.
Equity component, not interest.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Ordered priority list: primary gross interest expense first, then related components and net interest as fallback. Balance sheet items and minority interest lines are excluded per definition.

candidates.row_label: Remeasurement of previously held equity interest
candidates.why: This is a P&L remeasurement item, not a stock equity balance.
Represents income from affiliates, not total book equity.
Component of equity (contributed capital) but not total equity.
Subcomponent of paid-in equity; not total equity.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to shareholders of the parent
candidates.why: Represents equity attributable to parent shareholders only; useful but excludes non-controlling interests.
Represents total book equity including non-controlling interests; best match for BE definition.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to shareholders of the parent

variables.notes: Prefers total equity including minorities as BE. Parent-only equity and paid-in equity are secondary fallbacks.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to non-controlling interests; not the balance (stock) of minority interest, but related concept.
Duplicate concept to non-controlling interests on the income statement; again a flow, not the stock balance.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; directly represents minority equity interest (MIB).
Alternative label for the same balance sheet minority interest; also directly represents MIB.

variables.notes: Uses balance sheet minority interest lines for MIB, with both label variants in priority order. Income statement versions are flows and thus excluded.
